title: astragalus gulul-saranii
intro: Astragalus gulul-saranii, also known as Astragalus, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. Astragalus has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Chinese and Mongolian cultures, due to its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Astragalus is often prepared as a decoction, capsule, or tincture, and is commonly used to boost the immune system, reduce fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
